w:r.w:t: is a software project designed to provide a complete digital platform for managing the daily operations of a 4-star hotel. The main purpose of this project is to replace many manual hotel management processes with a more organized, efficient, and user-friendly system. Through this platform, the hotel can manage reservations, room availability, guest information, housekeeping activities, service requests, billing, payments, and reports in one centralized system.
The platform supports multiple types of users, including

. Each actor has specific functions based on their role in the hotel. Guests can register an account, log in, search for available rooms, view room details, make online reservations, cancel bookings, request additional services, report room issues, view their current bill, and submit reviews after their stay. This helps improve the guest experience by allowing customers to interact with the hotel more easily through an online system.

For hotel employees, the system provides useful tools to support daily work. Receptionists can manage booking lists, create walk-in reservations, modify or cancel reservations, check guests in and out, generate invoices, process payments, add extra charges, and view real-time room status. Staff members, especially housekeeping employees, can view assigned tasks, update room cleaning status, report room issues, and process guest service requests. These features help hotel operations become faster, clearer, and easier to track.

The platform also provides management and administration features. Managers can manage room information, room types, room pricing, maintenance blocks, housekeeping assignments, staff task progress, occupancy reports, revenue summaries, and reservation overviews. Admins can manage internal user accounts, configure system settings, view system activity logs, manage service and amenity catalogs, and access full revenue reports for higher-level decision-making.
Overall, the HMS platform is capable of supporting both

. It improves communication between guests and hotel staff, reduces manual paperwork, keeps room and booking information updated in real time, and provides useful reports for monitoring hotel performance. With its use case–driven design, this project creates a clear foundation for further system analysis, design diagrams, database design, and future software development.
Allows a new customer to create an account by entering required personal and login information. The system validates required fields and prepares the account for login.
Authenticates users with valid credentials and redirects them to the appropriate dashboard based on their role.
Allows a customer to start password recovery by providing account information such as email. The system verifies the request before reset.
Allows the customer to set a new password after the forgot-password verification step is completed successfully.
Determines the authenticated user role and redirects the user to the corresponding dashboard.
Displays room types or available rooms for browsing before login, including basic room information such as room name, amenities, capacity, and price range.
Shows detailed room information such as description, images, amenities, capacity, bed type, and nightly price. Login is required before making a reservation.
Displays available rooms or room types for browsing, including basic information such as room type, capacity, price, and availability status.
Allows the customer to search for available rooms by criteria such as date range, room type, capacity, or other booking conditions
Shows detailed information for a selected room or room type, including description, images, amenities, price, and reservation action.
Allows the customer to create an online reservation by selecting room, stay dates, guest information, and booking details.
Allows the customer to pay the required reservation deposit to confirm or secure the booking.
Allows the customer to cancel an eligible reservation according to the cancellation policy and current reservation status.
Displays the customer's previous and current reservations, including booking status, stay dates, payment information, and cancellation status.
Allows the customer to request hotel services during a stay, such as room service or support from hotel staff.
Allows the customer to report room-related issues such as broken equipment, missing amenities, or maintenance problems.
Allows the customer to submit feedback or ratings about the hotel, service quality, room experience, or stay experience.
Displays a list of reservations and bookings for receptionist review, filtering, and operational handling.
Shows complete information for a selected booking, including customer, room, date, payment, and booking status details.
Allows the receptionist to find bookings by customer information, booking code, room, date, or booking status.
Allows the receptionist to create a booking for a guest who arrives directly at the hotel without making an online reservation.
Allows the receptionist to update eligible booking details such as room, date, guest information, or related booking information.
Allows the receptionist to cancel a booking according to hotel policy and current booking state.
Allows the receptionist to record an offline deposit payment made at the counter for a booking.
Allows the receptionist to confirm guest arrival, verify booking information, and start the guest stay.
Allows the receptionist to assign an available room to a booking or active stay based on room availability and booking requirements.
Allows the receptionist to start guest check-out, confirm stay completion, and prepare inspection and billing activities.
Creates a request for housekeeping staff to inspect the room after check-out before finalizing billing.
Allows the receptionist to view housekeeping inspection results, including room condition and any reported damage or missing items.
Allows the receptionist to add approved extra charges to the guest bill, such as damage, minibar, or additional services.
Generates the final invoice based on room charges, deposits, services, additional charges, and payment status.
Allows the receptionist to record and confirm the final payment before completing the check-out process.
Allows the receptionist to view or update customer information needed for booking, check-in, stay management, or service handling.
Displays room status information such as available, occupied, dirty, cleaning, maintenance, or ready for assignment.
Allows the receptionist to receive, review, and route customer service requests to the appropriate staff group when needed.
Displays cleaning tasks assigned to housekeeping staff, including room, task status, priority, and assignment information.
Allows housekeeping staff to update progress or completion status of assigned cleaning tasks.
Displays service requests assigned to housekeeping staff for handling during guest stays or hotel operations.
Allows housekeeping staff to process assigned service requests and update the result or completion status.
Allows housekeeping staff to inspect a room after guest check-out and record the room condition.
Allows housekeeping staff to report damage, missing amenities, or maintenance issues found in a room.
Allows the manager to create, update, deactivate, or manage hotel room records and room operational status.
Allows the manager to manage room type information such as room type name, capacity, amenities, price, and description.
Allows the manager to create, assign, monitor, and manage tasks for housekeeping staff and technical staff.
Allows the manager to manage minibar items, including item information, price, and availability.
Allows the manager to view reservation-related operational information such as booking volume, upcoming stays, and reservation status summary.
Allows the manager to view revenue information summarized from bookings, payments, services, and hotel operations.
Allows the manager to view room occupancy information, such as how many rooms are occupied, available, or unused within a selected period.
Allows the manager to view and manage customer feedback to monitor service quality and support improvement decisions.
Allows the admin to create, update, activate, deactivate, or manage internal staff accounts for system access.
Allows the admin to reset passwords for internal staff accounts when employees forget passwords or access must be restored securely.
Allows the admin to manage system roles and role-related access control for internal users.
Allows the admin to view and update permitted admin profile information.
Customer: A customer who has registered an account on the system can book rooms, request services, make payments, and manage their personal information.
Housekeeping: Room attendants receive assigned cleaning tasks and update room status upon completion of duties.

Role 3: <<Receptionist: A front desk staff member responsible for receiving and processing reservation requests, handling check-in/check-out procedures, processing payments, and assisting guests throughout their stay.>>
Role 4:<<Manager: Manages the hotel, oversees all operational activities, reviews revenue and activity reports, manages staff, and handles customer feedback.>>
Role 5:<<Admin: System administrator, with full authority to manage user accounts, assign roles and permissions, and configure the system.>>
The API authenticates users via Google Account, returning a token to create a login session for the customer.
The service automatically sends emails (via Email Service) confirming bookings, payments, or system notifications to the Customer.
The API communicates with the Payment Gateway to process top-ups, confirm transactions, and return payment information to the receptionist.
Batch jobs automatically assign Housekeeping Task Information to the Housekeeping department based on room status (check-out, dirty, etc.).
Cron jobs periodically update room status (available/occupied/cleaning) based on booking data and Housekeeping Update Information.
The service listens for maintenance requests, automatically sends Maintenance Requests to Technical Staff, and updates Maintenance Information in the system.
Cron jobs run periodically (daily/weekly/monthly) to automatically compile and generate Activity Reports and Revenue Reports for managers.
The system automatically sends booking confirmation information (Booking Management Information, Room Information) to customers after successful payment.

Represents the permission group assigned to a user. Each role determines the user's access rights within the hotel management system, such as Guest, Receptionist, Housekeeping Staff, Maintenance Staff, and Manager.
Represents all users of the hotel management system. A user can make reservations, submit feedback, and perform system functions according to their assigned role.
Represents a room booking made by a user. It stores reservation information such as check-in/check-out dates, booking status, reserved room, requested services, and generated invoice.
Represents an individual hotel room available for reservation. Each room belongs to a room type and can be reserved by different reservations over time.
Represents a category of rooms with common characteristics such as room size, capacity, facilities, and standard pricing. Multiple rooms may belong to the same room type.
Represents additional hotel services available to guests during their stay, such as airport transfer, spa, laundry, restaurant, or room service. Services can be requested during a reservation.
Represents the billing information generated for a reservation. It summarizes room charges, requested services, taxes, and other applicable fees.

Represents a payment transaction made for an invoice. It records payment information including payment method, amount, transaction status, and payment time. Multiple payment records may exist if partial payments are supported.
Represents reviews or comments submitted by users after their stay. Feedback is used to evaluate service quality and improve customer satisfaction.
Customer, Receptionist, Admin, Manager, Housekeeping Staff, Technical Staff
Allows users to log in with an email and password. The system validates the credentials and grants access based on the user's role.
Customer, Receptionist, Admin, Manager, Housekeeping Staff, Technical Staff
Allows authenticated users to log out of the current session securely.

Allows the user to manage personal profile information. This includes viewing profile details, updating personal information such as full name, phone number, address, avatar, and email, and changing the account password when permitted.
Allows the customer to view the list of room types or available rooms displayed by the hotel, including basic information such as room name, capacity, amenities, and price range.
Allows the customer to view detailed room information, including description, images, amenities, capacity, bed type, and nightly price.
Allows the customer to make an online reservation by selecting an available room or room type, entering guest and contact information, reviewing booking details, and confirming the reservation.
Allows the customer to pay a reservation deposit after creating an online reservation. The system records the deposit and updates the booking payment status.
Allows the customer to cancel a previously created reservation before check-in according to the hotel's cancellation policy.
Allows the customer to view all personal reservations, including pending, active, completed, and canceled bookings.
Allows the customer to submit a rating and feedback about room quality, service quality, and overall hotel experience after check-out.
Allows the receptionist to view the daily booking list, including upcoming arrivals, current stays, and expected check-outs .
Allows the receptionist to view full details of a selected booking, including guest information, reserved room type, stay dates, deposit status, payment status, special requests, and booking status.
Allows the receptionist to search bookings by booking code, guest name, phone number, email, ID card number, passport number, date, or booking status.
Allows the receptionist to record a reservation deposit payment for bookings made at the front desk or through offline payment channels.
Allows the receptionist to check in a guest by finding the booking, verifying guest information, confirming stay details, assigning a room, and changing the room status to Occupied.
Allows the receptionist to assign a specific available room to a guest based on the reserved room type, stay dates, room availability, and guest requirements.

Allows the receptionist to check out a guest by confirming the stay completion, creating an inspection request, finalizing the bill, processing payment, and changing the room status to Dirty for cleaning.

Allows the receptionist to create a room inspection request after a guest checks out. The request is sent to housekeeping staff to inspect the room, check minibar or paid items, report damage, and prepare the room for cleaning.

Allows the receptionist to view the result of a room inspection, including room condition, damage report, minibar or paid item usage, lost items, and whether additional charges should be added to the bill.
Allows the receptionist to manually add a charge to a guest bill, such as minibar usage, damage fee, late check-out fee, or a special front-desk service.
Allows the receptionist to generate the official final invoice, including room charges, service fees, minibar charges, damage fees, deposits, and final payable amount.

Receptionist records the identity information of the representative guest for each room in the booking during check-in, including full name, contact details, ID/Passport number, and photos of the ID document, in order to verify guest identity and complete the check-in process.
Allows the receptionist to view a real-time room status board for all hotel rooms, including Available, Occupied, Dirty, Cleaning, and Maintenance.
Allows housekeeping staff to view assigned cleaning tasks for the current shift, including room number, task type, priority level, deadline, and task status.

Allows housekeeping staff to update cleaning task status, such as Not Started, In Progress, and Completed. The room status may also be updated from Dirty to Cleaning and from Cleaning to Available after cleaning is completed.

Allows housekeeping staff to inspect the room after guest check-out. This includes checking room condition, recording minibar or paid item consumption, recording lost or damaged items, reporting damage if found, and preparing the room for cleaning.

Allows housekeeping staff to report issues or damage found in a room, such as broken equipment, missing amenities, or required repairs. The system may change the room status to Maintenance and notify technical staff or the manager.

Allows the manager to manage hotel room records using CRUD-style operations. This includes adding new rooms, viewing room information, editing room details such as room number, floor, room type, capacity, amenities, images, and description, deactivating rooms that should not be reserved and deactivating rooms for maintenance during a defined period.

Allows the manager to manage system-wide room type categories using CRUD operations. This includes creating new room types, viewing room type details, editing room type information such as name, capacity, bed type, base price, amenities, and description, and deleting or deactivating room types that are no longer used.

Allows the manager to manage staff tasks. This includes creating and assigning tasks to housekeeping or technical staff, viewing staff task overview, updating task information such as priority or deadline, reassigning tasks when needed, and canceling or closing tasks that are no longer required.

Allows the manager to manage minibar items using CRUD operations. This includes adding new minibar items, viewing item details, editing item information such as name, category, price, stock status, and description, and deleting or deactivating items that are no longer available.
Allows the manager to view a reservation overview, including new bookings, current stays, upcoming arrivals, and upcoming check-outs by day or week.
Allows the manager to view operational revenue summaries by day, week, or month, including room revenue, service revenue, minibar revenue, and total revenue for business monitoring.

Allows the manager to manage customer feedback records. This includes viewing customer feedback, filtering feedback by rating or date, responding to customer feedback, updating response status, and archiving feedback that has already been handled. Feedback should usually be retained for historical service quality analysis instead of being permanently deleted.

Allows the admin to manage internal employee accounts using CRUD-style operations. This includes creating new internal accounts for staff, viewing account lists and account details, editing account information such as name, email, role, and active status, deactivating accounts that should no longer access the system.

Allows the admin to manage user roles and permissions. This includes creating roles, viewing role details, editing role names or permission sets, assigning roles to internal accounts, and deleting or deactivating roles that are no longer used.
Allows the admin to reset passwords for internal staff accounts when employees forget their passwords or when account access must be restored securely.
Allows the customer to reset the forgotten password in the Login Page.

Allows a new customer to create a personal account on the system. The user inputs required registration details such as full name, email, phone number, and password. The system validates the inputs, checks for email uniqueness, creates a new customer profile with the default "Customer" role, and secures the account setup.

Allows the manager to view and analyze hotel room occupancy statistics over a specified period (day, week, month, or year). The system calculates and displays key operational metrics, including the total number of occupied rooms, vacancy rates, room utilization statistics by room type.

Allows the manager to create, view, update, hide, show, and delete hotel policies displayed to customers. Each policy includes a title, policy group, content, and visibility status. The system automatically manages the display order so that active policies can be shown clearly on the customer policy screen.

Allows the customer to view active hotel policies published by the manager, including check-in time, check-out time, full payment requirement, cancellation rules, stay regulations, and feedback policy before or after making a reservation.

Allows the customer to update previously submitted feedback for a valid non-canceled booking. The system saves the previous feedback content into feedback history before updating the current feedback, so the feedback update history is preserved.
Allows the customer to view the manager’s response to submitted feedback. The system displays the feedback status and manager response if a response has been provided.

Allows the manager to respond to customer feedback. The manager reviews feedback details, enters a response, saves it, and the system updates the feedback status so the customer can view the response.
Receptionist, Admin, Manager, Housekeeping Staff, Technical Staff
User logs in with email and password to access the system.
User submits login form with credentials
User account exists in the system.
The user account was not locked by the admin.
User enters email and password and submits the login form.
System checks if the user exists.
The system checks if the account is locked and will block login to the system if necessary.
If credentials are valid and user is active, system generates Access Token and Refresh Token.
System sets refresh token in a secure cookie and returns access token and user info.
Error: If credentials are invalid, system returns an error.
Error: If user is not found, system returns an error.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Partial success: If the user's account is banned, the system will return an error.
User is authenticated and receives tokens, or receives an error message.
Allows a new customer to create a personal account in the system by providing required registration information.
User submits the registration form with personal and login information.
The user has not logged in yet
The email used for registration does not already exist in the system.
User enters required information such as full name, email, phone number, password, and confirm password.
System validates the required fields and checks the format of email, phone number, and password.
System checks whether the email already exists in the system.
System checks whether password and confirm password match.
If all information is valid, system encrypts the password.
System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
Receptionist, Admin, Manager, Housekeeping Staff, Technical Staff
User logs out of the system to end the current authenticated session securely.
User clicks the logout button or chooses the logout option from the system menu
User is already logged in to the system.
User has a valid active session or refresh token.
User clicks the logout button or selects the logout option.
System receives the logout request from the user.
System verifies the current user session or refresh token.
System removes or invalidates the refresh token from the server/session storage.
System clears the refresh token cookie from the browser.
System ends the current authenticated session.
System redirects the user to the login page or public home page.
System displays a successful logout message.
Error: If the session or refresh token is invalid, expired, or not found, the system clears local authentication data and redirects the user to the login page.
Error: If the system fails to invalidate the refresh token, the system still clears the client-side cookie and informs the user to log in again.
Error: If the logout request is unauthorized, the system rejects the request and redirects the user to the login page.
Partial success: If redirection fails, the system displays a message asking the user to reload the page or return to the login page manually.
The user is logged out successfully, the current session is ended, authentication tokens are removed or invalidated, and the user is redirected to the login page or public home page.
Allows the receptionist to view the daily booking list, including upcoming arrivals, current stays, and expected check-outs.
The receptionist opens the booking list screen to review daily hotel operations.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
Booking data is available in the system.
The system date or selected operating date is available.
Opens the booking list function from the receptionist workspace.
Displays bookings for the current date, including upcoming arrivals, current stays, and expected check-outs.
Selects a date, booking status, or list category if a filtered view is needed.
Refreshes the booking list based on the selected criteria.
Reviews guest name, booking code, room type, stay dates, deposit status, and booking status.
Selects a booking when more details or follow-up processing is required.
If no booking exists for the selected date or status, the system displays an empty list message.
If booking data cannot be loaded, the system shows an error message and keeps the previous screen state.
Allows the receptionist to view full details of a selected booking, including guest information, reserved room type, stay dates, deposit status, payment status, special requests, and booking status.
The receptionist selects a booking from a booking list or search result.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
The selected booking exists in the system.
Selects a booking to view its detailed information.
Retrieves the booking, guest, room, payment, and request information.
Displays booking code, guest information, reserved room type, stay dates, booking status, and special requests.
Displays deposit status, payment status, assigned room information, and related operational actions when available.
Reviews the information and decides whether to modify, cancel, check in, or return to the previous list.
If the booking no longer exists, the system shows a message and returns the receptionist to the booking list.
If some related information is unavailable, the system displays available details and marks missing sections clearly.
Allows the receptionist to check in a guest by finding the booking, verifying guest information, confirming stay details, assigning a room, and changing the room status to Occupied.
The guest arrives at the hotel and requests check-in for an existing booking.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
The guest has a valid booking eligible for check-in.
Guest identity information can be verified.
An available room can be assigned for the booking.
Searches for and selects the guest booking.
Displays booking details, guest information, reserved room type, stay dates, deposit status, and booking status.
Verifies guest identity and confirms stay details with the guest.
Checks whether the booking is eligible for check-in and whether deposit requirements are satisfied.
Assigns a specific available room or confirms the previously assigned room.
Updates the booking or stay status to Checked in.
Changes the assigned room status to Occupied.
Provides check-in confirmation and room information to the guest.
If no matching booking is found, the system shows a message and check-in cannot continue.
If guest identity cannot be verified, the receptionist stops check-in and asks the guest for valid information.
If the booking is canceled, completed, or not yet eligible for check-in, the system prevents check-in and shows the reason.
If a required deposit is unpaid, the system warns the receptionist and requires deposit handling before check-in if hotel policy requires it.
If no suitable room is available, the system asks the receptionist to assign another valid room or contact the manager for resolution.
The receptionist records the identity information of the representative guest for each room in the booking during the check-in process.
The receptionist proceeds to Step 2 (Guest Information) of the Check-in Wizard after Step 1 (Verify Condition) has passed.
The booking has passed condition verification (UC-011).
Each room in the booking has been assigned and is in "Ready" status.
displays the available account information of the booking owner (full name, phone number, email).
confirms whether the representative guest for the room is the booking owner or a different person.
captures or uploads photos of the front and back sides of the guest's ID document (National ID / Passport).
manually enters the ID/Passport number based on the physical document presented by the guest.
The receptionist checks the confirmation box declaring that the entered information has been personally verified against the physical document presented by the guest.
validates that all required fields (ID photos, ID number, confirmation checkbox) are completed for every room in the booking.
enables the "Next" button and proceeds to Step 3 (Assign Physical Room).
If the representative guest is not the booking owner, the receptionist selects "Enter different guest" and manually inputs the guest's full name and phone number.
Error: If one or both ID photos are missing for a room,
keeps the "Next" button disabled and highlights the missing photo(s).
Error: If the ID/Passport number field is empty,
keeps the "Next" button disabled and prompts the receptionist to enter it.
Error: If the confirmation checkbox is not checked, the system blocks progression to Step 3 until it is confirmed.
Identity information (full name, ID number, ID photos) is recorded for the representative guest of each room.
The booking is ready to proceed to Step 3 - Assign Physical Room.
The receptionist assigns a specific physical room to each room slot in the booking during check-in.
The receptionist proceeds to Step 3 (Assign Physical Room) of the Check-in Wizard after Step 2 (Guest Information) has been completed.
Guest information has been recorded for every room in the booking (UC-012).
At least one physical room in "Available" and "Clean" status exists for each booked room type.
For each room slot in the booking, the system displays a dropdown list of physical rooms that are Available, Clean, and match the booked room type.
The receptionist selects a physical room from the dropdown for each room slot.
Once a physical room is selected for a room slot, the system removes that room from the dropdown options of the remaining room slots.
The receptionist clicks "Next" after assigning a physical room to every room slot.
The system validates that every room slot has a distinct physical room assigned.
If validation passes, the system proceeds to Step 4 (Complete).
If the receptionist clicks "Next" while one or more room slots have no physical room selected, the system disables "Next" and highlights the missing selection(s).
Error: If two room slots are assigned the same physical room, system blocks progression and displays an error indicating the conflicting room code.
Error: If the physical room selected is no longer Available (e.g. taken by another concurrent check-in), system removes it from the list and prompts receptionist to reselect.
Each room slot in the booking is linked to a distinct, specific physical room.
The booking is ready to proceed to Step 4 - Complete.

Allows the receptionist to check out a guest by confirming the stay completion, creating an inspection request, finalizing the bill, processing payment, and changing the room status to Dirty for cleaning.
Guest requests check-out or the receptionist starts check-out for an active stay.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
The guest has an active checked-in booking or stay.
The assigned room and guest stay information are available.
The current bill can be calculated from room charges and service usage.
Searches for and selects the active booking or current stay.
Displays booking, guest, room, stay dates, deposit, and current bill information.
Confirms that the guest is ready to check out.
Creates a room inspection request for the assigned room.
Calculates the current payable amount based on room charges, services, deposits, and recorded charges.
Reviews the bill and confirms whether final payment should be processed.
After checkout confirmation, update the booking or stay status to Checked out.
Change the room status to Dirty so housekeeping can clean the room.
Error: If no active stay is found, the system shows a message and stops check-out.
Warning: If an inspection request already exists, the system links the existing request instead of creating a duplicate.
Warning: If payment is still pending, the system keeps the bill unpaid and prevents final completion when required by policy.
Error: If the room status cannot be updated, the system keeps the stay unchanged and informs the receptionist.
The stay is checked out, an inspection request is created or linked, and the room is marked Dirty; otherwise, the stay remains active and an error is shown.

Allows the receptionist to create a room inspection request after a guest checks out. The request is sent to housekeeping staff to inspect the room, check minibar or paid items, report damage, and prepare the room for cleaning.
submits an inspection request after or during guest check-out.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
The booking or room has just completed or is completing check-out.
The room exists and is assigned to the guest stay.
The room does not already have an active duplicate inspection request.
Selects the checked-out booking or room that requires inspection.
Displays room, guest, checkout time, and current room status.
Enters inspection notes, priority, and optional housekeeping assignment.
Validates the selected room and checks for duplicate active inspection requests.
Creates the room inspection request with Pending or Assigned status.
Sends the request to housekeeping staff for inspection and cleaning preparation.
Links the inspection request to the booking, room, and checkout record.
Error: If the room does not exist, the system rejects the request.
Error: If an active inspection request already exists, the system prevents duplicate creation.
Warning: If no housekeeping staff is available, the request remains Pending for later assignment.
Error: If required information is missing, the system asks the receptionist to complete the form.
A room inspection request is created and sent or queued for housekeeping; otherwise, no request is created and an error is displayed.

Allows the receptionist to view the result of a room inspection, including room condition, damage report, minibar or paid item usage, lost items, and whether additional charges should be added to the bill.
Receptionist opens the inspection result for a checked-out room or booking.
A room inspection request exists for the selected booking or room.
Housekeeping has submitted or updated the inspection result.
Searches for or opens the booking, room, or inspection request.
Retrieves the latest inspection result for the selected room.
Displays room condition, minibar usage, paid items, lost items, and damage details.
Reviews the inspection result and determines whether extra charges are needed.
Provides an option to continue to Add Charge to Bill when additional fees are required.
Closes the result view or proceeds to add charges.
Error: If no inspection request exists, the system informs the receptionist.
Warning: If inspection is still pending, the system shows Pending status and no final result.
Warning: If result data is incomplete, the system shows available information and marks missing fields.
If no charge is needed, the system allows the receptionist to continue checkout or invoice processing.
The receptionist has reviewed the inspection result and may add charges to the guest bill if necessary.
Allows the receptionist to manually add a charge to a guest bill, such as minibar usage, damage fee, late check-out fee, or a special front-desk service.
Receptionist selects Add Charge to Bill from a bill, booking, checkout, or inspection result.
A guest bill or booking exists.
The bill is not finalized as Paid or locked.
The charge type and amount are known
Opens the guest bill or selected booking.
Selects a charge type such as minibar, damage, late check-out, or service fee.
Enters amount, quantity, reason, and optional notes.
Validates required fields, charge amount, and bill status.
Adds the charge item to the bill.
Recalculates subtotal, taxes or fees if applicable, deposit deduction, and final payable amount.
Records the action for audit and displays the updated bill.
Error: If amount is invalid or negative, the system rejects the charge.
Error: If the bill is already paid or locked, the system prevents modification.
Warning: If a similar charge already exists, the system asks the receptionist to confirm or cancel.
Error: If saving fails, the bill remains unchanged and the system shows an error.
The bill is updated with the additional charge and the payable amount is recalculated; otherwise, no charge is added.
Allows the receptionist to generate the official final invoice, including room charges, service fees, minibar charges, damage fees, deposits, and final payable amount.
Receptionist chooses to generate the final invoice for a guest bill or checkout.
A guest booking or stay exists.
Room charges, service charges, deposits, and additional charges have been recorded.
The invoice has not already been finalized, unless regeneration is allowed.
Selects the booking or bill for invoice generation.
Collects room charges, service fees, minibar charges, damage fees, deposits, and discounts if any.
Calculates subtotal, deductions, and final payable amount.
Reviews invoice information and confirms generation.
Generates an invoice number and creates the official invoice record.
Sets invoice status to Unpaid or Pending Payment.
Displays the invoice for printing, exporting, or payment processing.
Warning: If inspection result is pending, the system warns that extra charges may not be complete.
Error: If calculation data is missing or inconsistent, the system stops generation.
Cancels invoice generation to update bill information first.
Error: If an invoice already exists and regeneration is not allowed, the system displays the existing invoice.
An official invoice is generated and ready for final payment; otherwise, no invoice is created.
Allows the receptionist to record final payment by cash, credit card, or bank transfer and update the invoice status to Paid.
Guest pays the final invoice amount and receptionist submits payment information.
The invoice has a payable amount greater than or equal to zero.
The selected payment method is supported.
Opens the final invoice for the guest.
Selects payment method: cash, credit card, or bank transfer.
Enters paid amount, transaction reference if any, and payment notes.
Validates invoice status, amount, and payment method.
Updates invoice status to Paid when the paid amount satisfies the payable amount.
Displays payment confirmation and receipt information.
Error: If the invoice is already Paid, the system prevents duplicate payment.
Error: If payment amount is invalid or insufficient, the system returns an error or keeps invoice unpaid.
Error: If card or bank transfer verification fails, the payment is not recorded as successful.
Error: If saving payment fails, the invoice status remains unchanged.
The final payment is recorded and the invoice is marked Paid; otherwise, payment is rejected and invoice status remains unchanged.

Allows the receptionist to manage guest profile records. This includes searching customer profiles, viewing customer profile details, creating or recording customer information during booking/check-in, and editing customer information when required. Historical customer records should not be hard-deleted by the receptionist.
Receptionist searches, views, creates, or edits customer profile information during booking or check-in.
- Customer information is available or can be entered.
The system can validate customer identity fields such as phone number, email, ID card, or passport
Searches for a customer profile by name, phone number, email, ID card, or passport.
Opens a profile to view details or chooses to create a new profile.
Creates or edits allowed customer information fields.
Validates required fields and checks for duplicate identity information.
Saves the new or updated customer profile.
Keeps historical records and displays the updated profile information.
If no profile is found, the system allows the receptionist to create a new profile.
Error: If duplicate email, phone, ID card, or passport is found, the system asks for confirmation or correction.
Error: If required information is missing or invalid, the system rejects the update.
Restriction: The receptionist cannot hard-delete historical customer records.
Customer profile information is viewed, created, or updated; historical customer data remains retained.
Allows the receptionist to view a real-time room status board for all hotel rooms, including Available, Occupied, Dirty, Cleaning, and Maintenance.
Receptionist opens the room status board from the front desk workspace.
Room records exist in the system.
The system has current room status information.
Loads all room records and their latest statuses.
Displays room number, room type, floor, current status, and related booking or task information if available.
Filters or sorts rooms by status, floor, room type, or availability.
Refreshes the board to show real-time updates such as Available, Occupied, Dirty, Cleaning, or Maintenance.
Selects a room to view more operational details if needed.
Error: If room data cannot be loaded, the system shows an error message.
Warning: If a room has conflicting status information, the system highlights the conflict.
Clears filters to return to the full room status board.
If real-time refresh fails, the system keeps the last loaded status and shows a warning.
Receptionist can see current room statuses and use the information for check-in, check-out, cleaning, and maintenance coordination.
Allows the receptionist to record a reservation deposit payment for bookings made at the front desk or through offline payment channels.
A guest pays a reservation deposit by cash, card, bank transfer, or another offline channel.
The receptionist is authenticated and authorized.
The booking exists and requires or accepts a deposit payment.
The payment amount and payment method are available.
Searches for and selects the booking that will receive a deposit payment.
Displays booking information, required deposit, paid deposit amount, and remaining deposit amount.
Enters payment amount, payment method, payment date, and transaction reference if available.
Validates the payment amount and payment details.
Saves the payment record and updates the booking deposit status.
Displays the updated deposit status and payment confirmation information.
If the booking is not found, the system shows an error message and stops the payment recording process.
If the amount is invalid or exceeds the allowed deposit amount, the system asks the receptionist to correct it.
If required payment reference information is missing, the system asks the receptionist to complete the payment details.

Allows the receptionist to receive, review, and handle customer service requests. This includes checking request details, assigning the request to suitable staff, tracking request status, and updating the request result when necessary.
A customer submits a service request or receptionist opens the customer service request list.
A customer service request exists or can be recorded.
Service catalog and staff assignment information are available
Opens the customer service request list.
Displays pending, assigned, in-progress, and completed service requests.
Selects a request and reviews customer, room, service type, priority, and notes.
Assigns the request to suitable housekeeping or technical staff when needed.
Updates request status to Assigned or In Progress and notifies the assigned staff.
Tracks request progress and updates the result when staff report completion.
Stores the request history and final status.
Error: If the request no longer exists or was canceled, the system shows the latest status.
Warning: If no suitable staff is available, the request remains Pending.
Error: If the selected staff role cannot handle the service type, the system rejects assignment.
If the request cannot be completed, receptionist records the reason and keeps the status Pending or Escalated.
The service request is reviewed, assigned, tracked, and updated; otherwise, it remains pending with a clear status.
Allows housekeeping staff to view assigned cleaning tasks for the current shift, including room number, task type, priority level, deadline, and task status.
The user is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff.
At least one cleaning task is assigned to the user
Retrieves assigned cleaning tasks from the database
Displays task information including room number, priority, deadline, and status
Reviews task information and selects a task if needed .
No data is modified in the system.
Task information becomes available for further processi

Allows housekeeping staff to update cleaning task status, such as Not Started, In Progress, and Completed. The room status may also be updated from Dirty to Cleaning and from Cleaning to Available after cleaning is completed .
Housekeeping Staff selects a cleaning task and clicks
The user is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff.
The cleaning task is assigned to the logged-in user.• Task status is Not Started or In Progress.
Opens a cleaning task from the task list.
Displays task details and room information.
Updates task status to In Progress.
Updates Room.Status from Dirty to Cleaning.
Updates task status to Completed and enters notes.
Updates Room.Status from Cleaning to Available.
Saves changes and displays a success notification.
Detects equipment damage or maintenance issues during cleaning.
Selects Report Room Issue and enters issue information.
Room.Status changes according to cleaning progress.
Allows housekeeping staff to view the list of pending service requests assigned to them, including request type, room number, priority, and deadline
• User is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff.
• Service requests exist in the system
Retrieves assigned service requests from the database.
Displays request information including room number, request type, priority, and status.
Selects a request for further processing if necessary.
No assigned service requests are found.
Room.Status changes according to cleaning progress.
Allows housekeeping staff to process additional service requests from guests, such as extra towels, pillows, room cleaning, or laundry support, and update the request status after completion
Housekeeping Staff selects a service request and clicks
• User is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff
.• Service request is assigned to the logged-in user.
• Service request status is Pending or Assigned.
• Related room information exists in the system
Opens the Service Request List screen.
Selects a service request from the list.
Reviews request information and required service.
Records completion history and displays a success notification.
Updates request status to Unable to Complete.
Records the reason and notifies Receptionist for further handling.
Customer request status is updated in real time.

Allows housekeeping staff to inspect the room after guest check-out. This includes checking room condition, recording minibar or paid item consumption, recording lost or damaged items, reporting damage if found, and preparing the room for cleaning
Housekeeping Staff opens a Room Inspection Request assigned after guest check-out
• User is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff.
Opens the assigned Room Inspection Request.
Displays room information and inspection form.
Inspects room condition, furniture, amenities, and inventory.
Verifies minibar items and room assets.
Creates a Housekeeping Cleaning Task for the room.
Records inspection history and displays a confirmation message.
Records minibar items and quantities consumed.
Creates InvoiceItem records and associates them with the guest invoice.
Notifies Receptionist to review the additional charges.
Detects damaged furniture, equipment, or room facilities.
Records damage information and supporting notes.
Inspection result is stored in the database
Inspection request status is updated to Completed.
Inspection information is available for Receptionists and Managers .

Allows housekeeping staff to report issues or damage found in a room, such as broken equipment, missing amenities, or required repairs. The system may change the room status to Maintenance and notify technical staff or the manager
button from a room task or room detail screen
User is authenticated as Housekeeping Staff.
The room exists in the system.
Housekeeping Staff has permission to report room issues
Displays the Room Issue Report form.
Enters issue category, description, and priority level.
Assigns the request to Technical Staff.
Sends notifications and displays a success message.
No alternative flow is defined for this use case
Customer change their current account password after logging in to the system.
Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.
Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.
User enters required information such as full name, email, phone number, password, and confirm password.
System validates the required fields and checks the format of email, phone number, and password.
System checks whether the email already exists in the system.
System checks whether password and confirm password match.
If all information is valid, system encrypts the password.
System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
Customer resets their forgotten password by verifying their account through email and creating a new password.
Customer submits the reset password form after receiving a valid reset password link or verification code.
Customer account exists in the system.
The reset password link or verification code is valid and not expired.
Customer opens the forgot password page from the login page.
Customer enters the registered email address and submits the request.
System checks whether the email exists in the system.
System generates a reset password token or verification code.
System sends the reset password link or verification code to the customer's email.
Customer opens the reset password link or enters the verification code.
System validates the reset token or verification code.
Customer enters new password and confirm new password.
Customer submits the reset password form.
System validates the required fields and password format.
System checks whether the new password and confirm new password match.
System updates the customer's password in the system.
System invalidates the used reset token or verification code.
System displays a successful password reset message and redirects the customer to the login page.
Error: If the email does not exist, system displays an error message or informs the customer that the request cannot be processed.
Error: If the email service fails, system displays a message asking the customer to try again later.
Error: If the reset token or verification code is invalid, system rejects the request and displays an error message.
Error: If the reset token or verification code is expired, system asks the customer to request a new password reset link.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If new password and confirm new password do not match, system displays an error message.
Error: If the new password does not meet security requirements, system asks the customer to enter a stronger password.
Error: If password update fails, system displays a password reset failure message.

The customer's password is reset successfully, the reset token or verification code becomes invalid, and the customer can log in using the new password. If the process fails, the password remains unchanged and the system displays an error message.
Customer views the list of available rooms or room types displayed by the hotel, including basic information such as room name, room type, capacity, amenities, price range, and availability status.
Customer opens the room list page from the homepage or customer dashboard.
Customer can access the public room list page
Room or room type data is available in the system.
Customer opens the room list page.
System retrieves room type or available room information from the database.
System displays the list of rooms or room types.
System shows basic room information such as room name, room type, capacity, amenities, price range, and availability status.
Customer may select a room or room type to view more details.
System redirects the customer to the selected room detail page.
Error: If room data cannot be loaded, system displays an error message.
If no rooms or room types are available, system displays an empty list message.
If some room information is missing, system displays available information and marks missing data clearly.
Customer leaves the room list page without selecting any room.
The customer successfully views the room list, or the system displays an error message if room information cannot be loaded.
Customer searches for available rooms by entering booking criteria such as check-in date, check-out date, room type, number of guests, and other filters.
Customer submits the room search form from the room search page or homepage.
Room information is available in the system.
Customer has entered valid search criteria..
The selected check-in date must be earlier than the check-out date.
Customer opens the room search page.
Customer enters search criteria such as check-in date, check-out date, room type, number of guests, and price range.
System checks room availability based on the selected date range.
System filters available rooms by room type, capacity, price, and other selected conditions.
System displays a list of available rooms or room types that match the search criteria.
Customer selects a room or room type to view more details.
System redirects the customer to the selected room detail page.
Error: If required search fields are missing, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the check-in date is later than or equal to the check-out date, system rejects the search and displays an error message.
If no rooms are available for the selected date range, system displays a no available rooms message.
If no rooms match the selected filters, system displays an empty result message and suggests changing the search criteria.
Error: If room data cannot be loaded, system displays an error message.
The customer successfully views a list of available rooms matching the search criteria, or the system displays an appropriate message if no rooms are available or the search criteria are invalid.
Customer views detailed information of a selected room or room type, including description, images, amenities, capacity, bed type, price, and availability status.
Customer selects a room or room type from the room list or search result.
Customer can access the room list or search result page.
The selected room or room type exists in the system.
Room detail information is available in the system.
Customer opens the room list page or search available rooms page.
Customer selects a room or room type to view details.
System receives the selected room or room type information.
System retrieves detailed room information from the database.
System displays room details such as room name, room type, images, description, capacity, bed type, amenities, price, and availability status.
Customer reviews the room detail information.
Customer may choose to make an online reservation for the selected room or return to the previous page.
Error: If the selected room or room type does not exist, system displays a not found message.
Error: If room detail data cannot be loaded, system displays an error message.
If some room information is missing, system displays available information and marks missing details clearly.
Customer returns to the room list or search result page without making a reservation.
The customer successfully views the selected room detail, or the system displays an appropriate error message if the room information cannot be found or loaded.
Customer makes an online reservation by selecting an available room or room type, entering stay dates, guest information, and confirming booking details.
Customer clicks the reservation button from the room detail page or available room search result.
Customer is logged in to the system.
The selected room or room type exists in the system. .
The selected room or room type is available for the chosen stay dates.
Customer has entered valid booking information.
Customer selects an available room or room type from the room detail page or search result.
Customer enters booking information such as check-in date, check-out date, number of guests, contact information, and special requests if any.
System checks whether the selected room or room type is still available for the selected date range.
System calculates the estimated booking price based on room price, stay duration, and selected options.
System displays the reservation summary for customer confirmation.
Customer reviews the reservation summary and confirms the booking.
System creates a new reservation record with Pending or Awaiting Deposit status.
System displays a successful reservation message and shows the next step for deposit payment.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
Customer changes their current account password after logging in to the system.
Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.
Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.
User enters required information such as full name, email, phone number, password, and confirm password.
System validates the required fields and checks the format of email, phone number, and password.
System checks whether the email already exists in the system.
System checks whether password and confirm password match.
If all information is valid, system encrypts the password.
System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
Customer changes their current account password after logging in to the system.
Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.
Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.
User enters required information such as full name, email, phone number, password, and confirm password.
System validates the required fields and checks the format of email, phone number, and password.
System checks whether the email already exists in the system.
System checks whether password and confirm password match.
If all information is valid, system encrypts the password.
System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.
Error: If required fields are missing or invalid, system displays a validation error message.
Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
Customer changes their current account password after logging in to the system.
Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.
Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.
User enters required information such as full name, email, phone number, password, and confirm password.
System validates the required fields and checks the format of email, phone number, and password.
System checks whether the email already exists in the system.
System checks whether password and confirm password match.
If all information is valid, system encrypts the password.
System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.

Error: If the email already exists, system rejects the registration and informs the user.
Error: Data validation failed. The system rejects those records and informs the manager.
Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.

Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.
Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.

System creates a new customer account with the default Customer role.
System displays a successful registration message and redirects the user to the login page.

Customer is already logged in to the system.
Customer account exists in the system.
Customer account is active and not locked.

Customer submits the change password form from the profile management screen.

Error: If the password does not meet security requirements, system asks the user to enter a stronger password.
Error: If account creation fails, system displays a registration failure message.
The new customer account is created successfully with the default Customer role, or the system displays an error message and the account is not created.
